description: PR6 commit 6 — chain-rotation stage telemetry. Mirrors pr5_chain_rotation.json: three shared-student sections form a bridge graph (CS101-CS102-CS103) so CS102 is the sole hotspot when all three seed the same slot. Test seeds all three at SUN 08:00 via ORM, then calls optimise_current_timetable with run_local_search=False and run_cpsat_polish=False so chain is the only stage that can move anything. With TIMETABLE_PR6_STAGE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=True, result.stage_telemetry.stage_ms.chain > 0 and chain.iterations == iterations attempted (matches the natural outer-loop counter, not accepted chains count). Other stage keys may stay zero at this commit scope.
notes: Test seeds all three sections at SUN 08:00 via ORM so CS102 becomes the unique hotspot.
chain-2 partners = {CS101, CS103}; chain search rotates CS102 + a partner off SUN and the score strictly improves.
chain.iterations counts the attempted outer-loop iterations of chain_local_search (matches the natural loop, not accepted-chains count).
Flag-off variant must leave all five stage keys at 0 even though the chain run emits PR5 decision-trace entries.
